title: Topology Declarations, Property Equivalence, Policies, and Ownership · Inference Brief: Durable Temporary Queue
summary: Multiple services declare the same queue with different durability and arguments, hardcode every operational x-argument, delete shared topology at startup, and turn precondition failures into reconnect loops. Planned artifact: Add and verify the durable temporary queue increment in versioned topology declaration and ownership manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose durable, exclusive server-named, auto-delete, and queue-TTL lifecycles from responsibility and race evidence.
Make repeated declarations equivalent in type, durability, exclusivity, auto-delete, and immutable arguments and diagnose precondition mismatch.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Durable Temporary Queue · Theory Durable Temporary Queue
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 8429 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to protect property equivalence and route mutable operations through policy or versioned migration; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then change one routing segment and prove the exact destination set. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
